vocabulary.definition: Tunnel Oxide Passivated Contact, a silicon solar cell technology using ultra-thin SiO2 layer with doped polysilicon for superior passivation
vocabulary.example: JinkoSolar achieved 26.66% certified efficiency on M10-sized TOPCon cells, narrowing the gap to theoretical limit.

vocabulary.definition: Multi-junction solar cell combining perovskite top cell with silicon bottom cell to surpass single-junction efficiency limits
vocabulary.example: The perovskite/TOPCon tandem cell achieved 32.76% certified efficiency with excellent operational stability.

vocabulary.definition: Dual-mode-coupled ligand used to regulate major organic cation (FA+) crystallization kinetics in perovskite films
vocabulary.example: The MBT ligand strategy enables effective control over crystallization kinetics on industrial thin silicon substrates.

vocabulary.definition: Algorithm or technique used in solar inverters to maximize power extraction from photovoltaic modules under varying conditions
vocabulary.example: The device maintained 91% of initial efficiency after 1,700 hours of continuous MPP tracking.

vocabulary.definition: Single layer of organic molecules that spontaneously assembles on a surface, often used to modify interface properties in solar cells
vocabulary.example: Conventional SAMs with flexible alkyl linkers are prone to molecular disorder under heat stress.

vocabulary.definition: Molecular design strategy constraining molecules both laterally and vertically to enhance interface stability
vocabulary.example: Multidimensional spatial confinement creates a 'molecular lock' at the interface for improved durability.

vocabulary.definition: Ratio of maximum power output to the product of Voc and Isc; indicates quality of solar cell performance
vocabulary.example: The inverted PSCs achieved 26.54% PCE with an impressive fill factor of 86.4%.

vocabulary.definition: Novel interface structure using core-shell nanoparticles to store and regulate charge carriers, suppressing defect evolution
vocabulary.example: The carrier nanocache structure with core-shell Si3N4 nanoparticles enables near-zero degradation in outdoor conditions.

vocabulary.definition: Interface between perovskite active layer and underlying substrate/electrode, critical for device stability and charge transport
vocabulary.example: Introducing Si3N4 nanocache at the buried interface significantly improves operational stability.

vocabulary.definition: Maximum voltage produced by a solar cell when current is zero, under illumination
vocabulary.example: The tandem cell achieved a high open-circuit voltage of 1.961 V.

vocabulary.definition: Percentage of sunlight energy converted to electrical energy by a solar cell
vocabulary.example: State-of-the-art perovskite single-junction cells have achieved PCE exceeding 27%.

vocabulary.definition: Electric current per unit area produced by a solar cell under short-circuit conditions
vocabulary.example: The target device exhibited an integrated Jsc of 32.55 mA cm-2 from EQE spectrum.

vocabulary.definition: Theoretical maximum efficiency for single-junction solar cells, approximately 33.7% for silicon
vocabulary.example: Tandem architectures bypass the Shockley-Queisser limit by stacking absorbers with different bandgaps.

vocabulary.definition: Perovskite material with formula FAPbI3, known for narrow bandgap and good thermal stability
vocabulary.example: FAPbI3 with optimal FA+/MA+ ratio achieves optimal phase stability and efficiency.

vocabulary.definition: Separation of halide components in mixed-halide perovskites under illumination, causing efficiency loss
vocabulary.example: MMSC-modified films show much slower phase separation due to suppressed ion migration.

vocabulary.definition: Recombination of electron-hole pairs without photon emission, causing energy loss in solar cells
vocabulary.example: Passivating grain boundaries effectively minimizes non-radiative recombination losses.

vocabulary.definition: Movement of ionic species within perovskite under electric field or heat, leading to instability
vocabulary.example: Suppressing ion migration is crucial for improving operational lifetime of perovskite devices.

vocabulary.chinese: 多晶硅/氧化硅/多晶硅结构
vocabulary.definition: Double-layer composite structure in TOPCon cells providing excellent passivation and contact properties
vocabulary.example: The polysilicon/silicon dioxide/polysilicon structure effectively prevents silver crystal penetration.

vocabulary.definition: Ratio of rear-side efficiency to front-side efficiency in bifacial solar cells
vocabulary.example: The optimized TOPCon cell achieved an impressive bifaciality of 88.3%.

vocabulary.definition: Study of crystal formation rate and mechanism during perovskite film deposition
vocabulary.example: Understanding crystallization kinetics is essential for controlling film quality and uniformity.

vocabulary.definition: Optimization strategy simultaneously addressing both front and rear electrical properties of solar cells
vocabulary.example: The dual-sided electrical synergy strategy achieved 26.66% efficiency on industrial TOPCon cells.

vocabulary.definition: Localized n-type polysilicon contacts on TOPCon cell front surface for reduced recombination
vocabulary.example: Patterned n-type finger contacts significantly reduce parasitic absorption and recombination losses.

vocabulary.chinese: 开尔文探针原子力显微镜
vocabulary.definition: AFM technique measuring surface work function to map electrical properties at nanoscale
vocabulary.example: KPFM measurements revealed enhanced interface work function with nanocache modification.

vocabulary.definition: Accelerated aging test cycling temperature between high and low extremes to evaluate device durability
vocabulary.example: Devices retained 94% of initial PCE after 200 thermal cycles between -40°C and 85°C.

vocabulary.chinese: 有机光伏稳定性国际峰会测试协议
vocabulary.definition: International Summit on Organic Photovoltaic Stability standardized testing protocols for solar cells
vocabulary.example: ISOS-L-1 protocol tests maximum power point tracking under 1-sun illumination.

vocabulary.definition: Duration a solar cell maintains acceptable performance under real operating conditions
vocabulary.example: Commercial deployment requires 25-year operational lifetime warranties.

vocabulary.definition: Environmental and health concern regarding lead content in perovskite solar cells
vocabulary.example: Regulatory barriers in EU and some US states may slow perovskite adoption due to lead toxicity concerns.

vocabulary.definition: Industrial manufacturing scale of gigawatt-level capacity per year
vocabulary.example: GCL Integration plans to scale to 5GW production capacity by end of 2026.

vocabulary.definition: Real-world performance evaluation of solar devices in actual environmental conditions
vocabulary.example: Six-month outdoor field testing in Beijing demonstrated near-zero degradation.

vocabulary.definition: Average time electron-hole pairs survive before recombination, critical for device efficiency
vocabulary.example: Ultra-long carrier lifetimes exceeding 18 μs were achieved through hetero-polytypic perovskite design.

vocabulary.chinese: 晶体溶剂化物成核策略
vocabulary.definition: Low-dimensional crystal-solvate materials used as buried-interface nucleation layers that encapsulate solvent molecules within crystal lattice and release them slowly during annealing
vocabulary.example: The CSV seeding strategy transforms interfacial crystallization from violent and uncontrolled to gentle and programmable.

vocabulary.definition: Solvent molecules encapsulated within crystal lattice that are gradually released during film annealing, enabling gentle and controlled interfacial crystallization
vocabulary.example: The slow-release solvent function eliminates interfacial voids and flattens grain-boundary nanogrooves.

vocabulary.chinese: 接触触发阳离子相互作用
vocabulary.definition: Molecular interaction triggered when two perovskite films are placed in physical contact, reorganizing crystal structure throughout the material depth
vocabulary.example: CCI reorganizes the light-absorbing layer at molecular level without adding any extra chemicals.

vocabulary.definition: Three-dimensional intertwined structure formed by integrating nanoparticles with molecular layers, transforming planar interfaces into 3D interconnected networks
vocabulary.example: The SiOx-Py3 bulk nano-heterojunction architecture fundamentally prevents molecular disorganization.

vocabulary.definition: Strong covalent bonding (P-O-Si) between functional molecules and nanoparticle scaffolds that 'welds' molecules firmly to the nanobackbone
vocabulary.example: Molecular welding via P-O-Si bonds with -10.89 eV binding energy creates robust interface structures.

vocabulary.definition: Structural defect regions where material structure is slightly altered; forms interconnected networks that act as charge separation highways in perovskites
vocabulary.example: Domain walls function as 'highways for charge carriers', allowing charges to travel long distances efficiently.

vocabulary.definition: Optical technique using dichroic mirrors to separate light wavelengths and direct them to optimized subcells in multi-junction devices
vocabulary.example: Spectral splitting enables flexible combinations of solar cells with wide range of compositions in 4T tandem configuration.

vocabulary.definition: Molecules that undergo reversible conformational changes under light/dark conditions, used to dynamically dissipate mechanical stress at grain boundaries
vocabulary.example: Azobenzene derivative Ca-Abz acts as 'molecular spring' to absorb and release strain energy via reversible molecular deformation.

vocabulary.definition: Novel organic-cesium compound designed for efficient Cs+ doping and uniform cation distribution in perovskite films
vocabulary.example: CsDPPBA enables efficient cesium doping while improving phase stability of FAPbI3-based perovskites.

vocabulary.definition: Advanced characterization technique combining atomic force microscopy with infrared spectroscopy to map chemical signatures at nanoscale
vocabulary.example: PiFM confirmed uniform bond formation throughout perovskite film depth at the CCI interface.

vocabulary.chinese: 非晶态混合卤素中间相
vocabulary.definition: Disordered intermediate phase formed during perovskite crystallization that prevents long-range periodic arrangement and enables uniform halide distribution
vocabulary.example: PDI forms amorphous intermediate phase, ensuring synchronous precipitation of iodide and bromide during annealing.

vocabulary.definition: Average time charge carriers (electrons and holes) survive before recombining; critical parameter for solar cell quality
vocabulary.example: CCI treatment increased charge carrier lifetime from 4.48 to 5.89 microseconds.
